In paragraph (1), the words “wholly owned by citizens of the United States for purposes of engaging in the coastwise trade” are substituted for “wholly owned by a person who is a citizen of the United States within the meaning of the laws respecting the documentation of vessels” for consistency in this chapter.Subsection (a)(2) is substituted for “having in force a certificate of documentation issued under ” for consistency in this chapter and with section 12102(b) as revised by the bill.section 12106 of title 46In subsection (b)(1), the words “in the United States to which the coastwise laws apply” are substituted for “in the United States, its Territories or possessions, embraced within the coastwise laws of the United States” because of the definition of “United States” in chapter 1 of the revised title and because of section 55101 of the revised title.In subsection (b)(3), the words “or place” are omitted as surplus. See 19 C.F.R. § 4.92 (2004). children.text: is wholly owned by citizens of the United States for purposes of engaging in the coastwise trade; and

has been issued a certificate of documentation with a coastwise endorsement under chapter 121 of this title or is exempt from documentation but would otherwise be eligible for such a certificate and endorsement.
a vessel between ports or places in the United States to which the coastwise laws apply, either directly or via a foreign port or place;
a vessel from point to point within the harbors of ports or places to which the coastwise laws apply; or

a vessel transporting valueless material or dredged material, regardless of whether it has commercial value, from a point in the United States or on the high seas within the exclusive economic zone, to another point in the United States or on the high seas within the exclusive economic zone.

children.heading: .—Owner and master
children.label: 1 .—Owner and master
children.text: The owner and master of a vessel towing another vessel in violation of this section are each liable for a penalty of at least $350 but not more than $1,100. A penalty under this paragraph constitutes a lien on the vessel. The lien is enforceable in a district court of the United States for any district in which the vessel is found. Clearance may not be granted to the vessel until the penalties have been paid.

children.heading: .—Vessel
children.label: 2 .—Vessel
children.text: In addition to the penalties under paragraph (1), the towing vessel is liable for a penalty of $60 per ton based on the tonnage of each towed vessel.
